ogTitle: Cams Wharf vs Canada Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cams Wharf and Canada Bay.
heading: Cams Wharf vs Canada Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cams Wharf, New South Wales and Canada Bay, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Canada Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Cams Wharf (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cams Wharf or Canada Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cams Wharf has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Canada Bay scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cams Wharf scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Canada Bay scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cams Wharf or Canada Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Canada Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
